sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I understand the mechanics much better:
Step 12 had 4 green crosses (N) and 4 gray nodes (g):
- 2 green crosses in horizontal section
- 2 green crosses in vertical section
- The gray nodes formed a selection frame around one of the horizontal crosses (the one at [[18, 36], ...])
- There was also a gray target at [[18, 42], [18, 43]]
After clicking at (20, 43) which was inside the selection frame:
- The selected cross moved to the target position [[18, 42], [18, 43]]
- The other cross that was at [[18, 30], ...] disappeared (merged or was replaced)
- Now there are 3 green crosses total: one in horizontal (at the target position), two in vertical
So the mechanic is:
1. Click a green cross to select it (G frame appears)
2. A gray target appears somewhere
3. Click inside the selection frame to move the cross to the target
Now in step 13 (current), there are 3 green crosses and no gray targets/frames. I need to:
1. Click one of the green crosses to select it
2. See where the target appears
3. Click the selection frame to move it
Let me click on one of the green crosses. The one in the horizontal section is at boundary [[18, 42], [18, 43], [19, 44], [20, 44], [21, 43], [21, 42], [20, 41], [19, 41]]. The center would be around row 19-20, col 42-43.
Let me click on the center of the first green cross (horizontal section).
